untranslatable_reason: Signal requires monitoring grain prices as a separate asset (external data source not in allowed indicators list). The rule is a cross-asset spread strategy (grain → cattle futures) that cannot be expressed using single-instrument technical indicators. 'Grain prices move higher' requires grain price data, which is not available in the allowed indicator set. Additionally, the spread structure (sell nearby + buy deferred) requires position management logic beyond entry/exit conditions.
translation_notes: This is a fundamental inter-commodity spread strategy that depends on external grain price data and position coordination across two futures contracts. A tradeable version would require: (1) grain price feed as external input, (2) separate entry rules for each leg, (3) spread-specific exit logic. The provided indicators cannot capture the grain→cattle relationship. Attempted to approximate with a generic short cattle signal using price mean reversion, but this misses the core economic logic of the pattern.
